title: Meta-Analysis of JRN Probe Reliability: Heterogeneity Decomposition and Practitioner Guidelines
summary: Demo notebook performing a meta-analysis of JRN (Join Reproduction Number) probe reliability across 7 experiments and 4 datasets. Uses 63 curated records from mini_demo_data.json to compute: pooled JRN statistics (mean=1.21, 77.8% beneficial), probe-vs-GT error metrics (30 pairs, sign agreement=1.0, median abs error=0.08), GBM meta-model with LODO-CV, depth-2 decision rules (80% accuracy), training-free proxy reliability for 5 proxies with per-dataset Spearman rho and selection AUC, proxy selection decision tree (95.2% accuracy), and heterogeneity decomposition (I²=98.1%, Q=155.91, τ²=1.85). Includes 4-panel visualization: JRN distribution by dataset, probe-vs-GT scatter, proxy reliability comparison, and forest plot of study correlations.
